channel.title: Pratik Dhanave — Blog
channel.description: Long-form writing on multi-agent AI, medical AI governance, HIPAA-aware architecture, and cloud-native systems.

item.title: Human-in-the-Loop: An Approval Gate on Durable State
item.description: Series finale, Lesson 7: a sensitive action pauses for human approval, where suspension is just a Lesson 2 checkpoint marked awaiting_approval, the deadline is checked first so a late yes is void, and the action must be idempotent.

item.title: Hierarchical Supervision: Bounded Fan-Out, Ordered Fan-In, Fault Isolation
item.description: Lesson 6: bounded fan-out behind a semaphore, ordered fan-in via a pre-sized results slice, and per-worker fault isolation so one sub-agent panicking becomes one failed result instead of crashing the whole run.

item.title: Orchestration and Handoff: Routing Intent to a Specialist
item.description: Lesson 5: a triage router first-matches a keyword to hand intent to a specialist, and the exact point a substring table stops being able to think.

item.title: Advanced Memory: Threads, Keyword Retrieval, and Lossy Summarization
item.description: Lesson 4: memory is three stores, not one — an append-only thread, a keyword knowledge index, and a lossy first-and-last summarizer — and an honest account of where each local stand-in leaks against Azure.

item.title: Google ADK Glossary: Every Core Concept in One Place
item.description: The capstone of the series: every core ADK concept defined in one place — agents and orchestration, tools, sessions/state/memory, context and callbacks, runtime and streaming, models, grounding, evaluation, protocols, and deployment.

item.title: Secure Sandboxing: Running Agent-Written Code Behind a Timeout
item.description: Lesson 3: run agent-written code behind a hard timeout with exec.CommandContext, distinguish OK from TimedOut, and face the leak — a subprocess is not a security boundary.

item.title: Agent Config: Defining an ADK Agent in YAML, No Code Required
item.description: Defining an agent declaratively in YAML and loading it via from_config — the loader reads, resolves, and validates the config into a built agent, so config-as-data works without writing code.

item.title: Durable Execution: Checkpoint Every Step, Resume After a Crash
item.description: Lesson 2: a workflow that checkpoints after every step and resumes from the last one after a crash, why at-least-once execution forces idempotent steps, and the atomic-rename store that survives a killed process.

item.title: Context Caching in ADK: Stop Paying for the Same Tokens Every Turn
item.description: Context caching cuts latency and cost by caching large, stable context — system prompt, reference docs, tool definitions — so repeated calls don't re-send and re-process the same tokens.

item.title: The LangGraph Glossary: Every Concept in One Place
item.description: The capstone of the series: every LangGraph concept defined in one place — the graph model, state and reducers, persistence, human-in-the-loop, agents and tools, parallelism, streaming, and the surrounding ecosystem.

item.title: The Agent Harness: guardrails as middleware around the model
item.description: Lesson 1: why the input guardrail is a hard block rather than flag-and-pass, why it counts runes instead of bytes, and how a plain net/http handler wraps the (stubbed) model call so it tests with httptest.

item.title: Planners & Thinking: Making an ADK Agent Reason Before It Acts
item.description: Structuring an agent's reasoning: planners that make the model plan-then-act (ReAct-style), the built-in thinking feature, and how a planner improves multi-step tool use over naive prompting.

item.title: Human-in-the-Loop, Tools, and the Send API
item.description: The building blocks on top of the core graph: interrupt() to pause for human input, create_react_agent and ToolNode for tool-using agents, and the Send API for dynamic parallel fan-out with a reducer fan-in.

item.title: AG-UI State Management: The Server
item.description: The server side of AG-UI state management: middleware emits a DataContent state snapshot from the model's JSON so the client can adopt shared state across turns.

item.title: Harness Engineering in Go: build the harness, then let Azure supply it
item.description: Seven patterns that turn a bare model call into production agent infrastructure, each written first as offline Go behind an interface (the seam) so the leap to Azure is a swap, not a rewrite.

item.title: Google ADK Code Execution: Let the Agent Run the Code, Not Guess the Answer
item.description: Letting an agent write and run code: built-in and container-based code executors, safe sandboxed execution, how results flow back into the conversation, and the security tradeoffs.

item.title: Checkpointing and Persistence: Pause, Resume, Time-Travel
item.description: A checkpointer saves state at every superstep boundary, so a run can pause, resume on a thread_id, and even fork from an earlier checkpoint (time-travel). This is the foundation human-in-the-loop is built on.

item.title: AG-UI Human-in-the-Loop: The Server
item.description: The server side of AG-UI human-in-the-loop: gate a tool with tool.ApprovalRequiredFunc so the server suspends the run and resumes on the client's decision.

item.title: Skills: Packaging Agent Capabilities as Reusable, Discoverable Bundles
item.description: Skills package reusable capabilities — instructions, tools, resources, including file-based skills — so they can be discovered and attached to agents, promoting reuse across projects.

item.title: Streaming: values, updates, and debug Modes
item.description: stream() exposes a run in three modes: values (full state after each node), updates (what each node changed), and debug (the raw event stream). Streaming falls out naturally from the superstep model.

item.title: AG-UI Frontend Tools: The Server
item.description: The server side of AG-UI frontend tools: set DisableFuncAutoCall so the server forwards tool calls to the client to execute and awaits the result.

item.title: Hosting and the Capstone App — MAF in Go
item.description: Wrap Go agents in A2A, MCP, and AG-UI transports, then run the end-to-end client/server capstone where a host agent uses remote specialists as tools.

item.title: Plugins in Google ADK: One Hook, Every Agent
item.description: Plugins are cross-cutting hooks that apply globally across every agent, tool, and runner — logging, policy, metrics, caching — as opposed to per-agent callbacks. When a plugin beats a callback.

item.title: Command: Update State and Pick the Next Node in One Return
item.description: Command lets a node return a state update and a goto in one object, moving the routing decision inside the node. It is the cleanest way to express supervisor handoffs and dynamic control flow.

item.title: AG-UI Backend Tools: The Server
item.description: The server side of AG-UI backend tools: attach server-owned function tools to the Foundry agent so the tool round-trip stays entirely server-side.

item.title: Advanced Workflows — MAF in Go
item.description: Durable MAF workflows in Go: checkpoint and rehydrate a fresh graph, pause on a RequestPort for a human, nest sub-workflows, and coordinate via scoped shared state.

item.title: Models in Google ADK: One Agent, Any Backend
item.description: ADK is model-agnostic: use Gemini natively or plug in Claude, GPT, or Ollama via LiteLLM and a model registry, swapping the model without changing agent code.

item.title: Cycles and the Agent Loop: Branch, Act, Loop Back
item.description: Branching plus a back-edge is a cycle, and that cycle IS the agent loop: model proposes tool calls, tools run, control returns to the model, repeat until done. Plus recursion_limit, the guardrail that stops a runaway loop.

item.title: AG-UI Getting Started: The Server
item.description: The AG-UI server half: wrap a Foundry agent in aguiprovider.NewJSONHTTPHandler and serve it over HTTP/SSE for any AG-UI client to drive.

item.title: Orchestration Patterns — MAF in Go
item.description: The Sequential, Concurrent, and Group Chat orchestration builders in agent-framework-go, plus wrapping a whole workflow as one nestable agent.

item.title: Grounding & RAG in ADK: Answers Anchored in Real Data
item.description: Grounding answers in real data: retrieval tools, grounding metadata returned with responses, rendering citations from that metadata, and the retrieve-augment-generate RAG pattern in ADK.

item.title: Conditional Edges: Routing with a Function and a Path Map
item.description: A conditional edge is a router function plus a path map: the router reads state and returns a key, the path map turns that key into the next node. This is how branching (and, next post, loops) are expressed.

item.title: Capstone · DocQA — answer questions about your own documents
item.description: The capstone: a grounded DocQA agent that answers only from embedded docs via a search_docs tool, cites sources, and refuses to guess — with an optional reviewer.

item.title: Workflows with Agents — MAF in Go
item.description: Agents as Go workflow executors: switch routing, fan-out and fan-in barriers, and mixing typed function nodes with agent nodes in one graph.

item.title: Protocols in Google ADK: MCP for Tools, A2A for Agents
item.description: Two interoperability protocols: MCP lets an agent consume tools from external servers, and A2A — HTTP for agents — lets one agent discover and call another remote agent as a peer over HTTP.

item.title: Edges, START, END, and compile(): Wiring and Running a Graph
item.description: Edges, START, END, compile() and invoke() are the four smallest pieces that turn a bag of nodes into a runnable program. Here is the full lifecycle of a tiny two-node graph.

item.title: A2A Server
item.description: Host one Foundry agent over A2A: newMux pins the card interface URL, wraps the agent in an a2aprovider executor, and serves the card plus JSON-RPC routes.

item.title: Workflow Mechanics — MAF in Go
item.description: The MAF workflow model in Go: executors bound to IDs, AddEdge wiring, WithOutputFrom, and typed WatchStream events - plus an upstream route-builder fix.

item.title: Safety & Security in Google ADK: Defense in Depth
item.description: Layered defense-in-depth for agents: input/output guardrails via callbacks, Gemini safety settings, restricting tools, and sandboxing untrusted actions.

item.title: Nodes: Functions That Read State and Return Partial Updates
item.description: A node is just a function: it receives the whole current state and returns only the channels it changed. Understand the partial-update contract and the immutable-snapshot guarantee that makes supersteps safe.

item.title: A2A Client
item.description: A Foundry host agent resolves remote A2A agent cards and turns each remote agent into a callable tool with agenttool.New over the a2aprovider.

item.title: Observability, Safety, and Providers — MAF in Go
item.description: Wrap every MAF run in an OpenTelemetry span, gate risky tool actions behind a permission handler, and swap Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, Copilot, and Azure behind one agent.

item.title: Observability in ADK: Seeing Inside a Running Agent
item.description: Seeing inside a running agent: OpenTelemetry tracing with spans for agent, model, and tool steps, structured logging, and exporting traces to debug latency and tool-call trajectories.

item.title: State, Channels, and Reducers: How LangGraph Merges Updates
item.description: State is a typed dict of channels; each channel has an optional reducer. No reducer overwrites; a reducer (like add_messages or operator.add) combines. This is the single idea the rest of LangGraph is built on.

item.title: subworkflows · Nested Order Processing
item.description: inproc.BindSubworkflowAsExecutor binds a whole workflow as one node; an order pipeline nests Payment and FraudCheck two levels deep, and inner events still bubble up.

item.title: Middleware — MAF in Go
item.description: A guardrail middleware that blocks an MAF Go run before the model, chained ahead of a logger — plus the tool-approval nil-update panic I fixed upstream in PR #472.

item.title: Deploying an ADK Agent: One Command to Cloud Run and Agent Engine
item.description: Deploying an agent: adk deploy with its cloud_run and agent_engine subcommands, containerizing the app, and reliability rules like ack-after-invocation so failures are redelivered, not dropped.

item.title: LangGraph Is a Pregel Program: Shared State vs Message Passing
item.description: LangGraph is shared-state, not message-passing, and both models descend from Google's Pregel/BSP: work advances in supersteps that end at a synchronization barrier. Get this mental model first and the whole API stops being magic.

item.title: shared-states · Coordinating executors through shared state
item.description: One executor stores a document with QueueStateUpdate; fan-out counters read it back with ReadState, and an AddFanInBarrierEdge aggregates once both deliver. Offline.

item.title: Shaping a Run — MAF in Go
item.description: Decode typed structs from a run, drain a streamed response by ranging it, and send an image with RunMessage and DataContent.
